# RFC-NNNN: <Proposal title>

- **Status:** Draft | In review | Accepted | Rejected | Implemented
- **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
- **Author:** <role>

An RFC is for designs that span multiple ADRs or need narrative explanation —
a new module, a cross-cutting mechanism (sync, permissions, automation), a
public API surface. ADRs record single decisions; RFCs design systems.

## Summary

Three sentences maximum. What is proposed and what it enables.

## Motivation

The product or engineering problem. Link PRD sections.

## Detailed design

The full design: data model, API surface, module boundaries, failure modes,
migration story. Diagrams (Mermaid) encouraged.

## Security considerations

Tenant isolation, authorization, input validation, audit trail, abuse cases.

## Performance considerations

Expected load, query patterns, caching, worst cases.

## Alternatives

What else was considered and why this design won.

## Rollout plan

Phasing, feature flags, migration, rollback.

## Unresolved questions

What is deliberately left open, and who/what will resolve it.
